generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance HVACControllerActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System actuator for HVAC systems enabling automated adjustments based on real-time data and control logic"
]

instance LightingSystemActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System actuator for lighting systems enabling automated adjustments based on occupancy and energy optimization requirements"
]

instance StructuralLoadActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuator component for structural system adjustments in response to real-time stress analysis and predictive maintenance requirements"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance EnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT sensor measuring real-time energy consumption across building systems"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

instance StructuralIntegritySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT sensor network monitoring structural health through vibration analysis and material stress detection"
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralIntegrityDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT sensor detecting occupancy patterns and space utilization metrics"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT sensor network measuring temperature, humidity, and air quality parameters"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalConditionDataTransmitted
]

instance OperationalLogSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "System logging component capturing operational metrics and system performance logs"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OperationalLogDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

instance StructuralIntegrityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralIntegritySensor
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalConditionSensor
]

instance OperationalLogDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OperationalLogSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BIMGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Building Information Model providing static geometric representation of building assets and subsystems"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMToDTTransformationEnabler
]

instance BCSBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Building Control System behavioral model representing dynamic interactions between sensors, actuators, and control logic"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BCSIntegrationEnabler
]

instance EnergyPerformanceSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "High-fidelity simulation model for energy performance analysis and optimization scenarios"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationExecutionEnabler
]

instance StructuralAnalysisModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Structural analysis model for stress testing and lifecycle performance prediction"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StructuralAnalysisEnabler
]

instance EnergyConsumptionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time and historical energy consumption data aggregated from building systems"
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler, PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler
]

instance StructuralIntegrityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structural health monitoring data including vibration patterns and material stress metrics"
    DTDFVocab:fromData StructuralIntegrityDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StructuralAnalysisEnabler
]

instance OccupancyPatternData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Space utilization and occupancy pattern data for facility management optimization"
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SpaceUtilizationEnabler, EnergyOptimizationEnabler
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time environmental parameters including temperature, humidity, and air quality measurements"
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnvironmentalConditionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HVACControlEnabler
]

instance OperationalLogData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical and real-time operational logs capturing system performance and maintenance records"
    DTDFVocab:fromData OperationalLogDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AnomalyDetectionEnabler, MaintenanceSchedulingEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance BIMToDTTransformationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering framework enabling automatic extraction of structured representations from enriched BIM models"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, VisualizationService
]

instance BCSIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [

    DTDFVocab:enables ControlAndAutomationService
]

]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [

]

instance SimulationExecutionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [

]

instance StructuralAnalysisEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [

    DTDFVocab:enables StructuralAnalysisService
]

instance HVACControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [

]

instance SpaceUtilizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Space planning and utilization optimization tools"

]

instance EnergyOptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Energy efficiency optimization algorithms and decision support systems"
    DTDFVocab:enables EnergyOptimizationService
]

instance AnomalyDetectionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Anomaly detection algorithms for identifying operational deviations and potential failures"
    DTDFVocab:enables AnomalyDetectionService
]

instance MaintenanceSchedulingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance scheduling system based on asset condition data"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveMaintenanceService
]

instance EclipseHonoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Lightweight M2M protocol for real-time IoT data exchange with low-latency communication"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance EclipseDittoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Device management framework enabling dynamic modeling of physical assets as software entities"
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlAndAutomationService
]

instance UnrealEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/4D visualization engine for immersive building representations and simulations"
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Aggregates sensor data and operational logs to provide live performance metrics for building systems"
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingPerformanceInsight, AnomalyAlertAction
]

    DTDFVocab:provides MaintenanceRecommendationInsight, MaintenanceScheduleAction
]

instance SimulationAndOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [

]

]

]

instance CollaborativePlatformService : DTDFVocab:Service [

]

]

]

]

// C1: Environment
instance AECOBuildingDigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Cyber-physical infrastructure environment for AECO buildings spanning entire lifecycle from design to operation"
    base:contains BIMGeometricModel, BCSBehavioralModel, EnergyPerformanceSimulationModel,
                StructuralAnalysisModel, EnergyConsumptionData, StructuralIntegrityData,
                OccupancyPatternData, EnvironmentalConditionData, OperationalLogData,
                HVACControllerActuator, LightingSystemActuator, StructuralLoadActuator,
                EnergyConsumptionSensor, StructuralIntegritySensor, OccupancySensor,
                EnvironmentalConditionSensor, OperationalLogSensor
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT system enables **control flows** from the digital twin to physical infrastructure, primarily through **runtime interfaces** that facilitate bidirectional communication. Facility Managers interact with the deployed DT to issue operational commands (e.g., HVAC adjustments, lighting controls, or equipment activation), though specific **command types** or **message formats** are not detailed. The framework supports **validation and authentication** mechanisms implicitly via its **accessibility** requirements, ensuring only authorized users (e.g., Facility Managers) can execute commands. Feedback systems are inferred through the **real-time data exchange** loop, where physical responses (e.g., sensor confirmations) are relayed back to the DT for monitoring. Conflict resolution is not explicitly described, but the **modular architecture** and **runtime configuration management** suggest dynamic adjustments to mitigate discrepancies. Command execution confirmation is implied by the system’s **holistic life cycle management**, though no explicit acknowledgment protocol is mentioned."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure **continuous synchronization** between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, enabling dynamic operational monitoring and control. The framework supports **low-latency interactions** as a core requirement, though specific latency metrics (e.g., sub-second or millisecond-level thresholds) are not explicitly defined. Synchronization is bidirectional, facilitating immediate updates between the physical and digital domains. The system is designed to accommodate **operational time scales** relevant to building management, including real-time adjustments for facility managers and control engineers, without specifying periodic batch updates or event-driven granularity beyond the general need for **continuous synchronization** as a foundational requirement."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs a **modular, service-oriented architecture** with **orchestration-driven coordination**, structured around a **central DT Framework** that facilitates bidirectional data exchange and life-cycle management. Key orchestration mechanisms include:
  - **Role-based workflows**: *Building Control Engineers* initiate deployment via BIM tools, *Facility Managers* handle real-time operations, and *DT Engineers* integrate custom services, creating a **pipeline from enriched BIM models to operational DTs**.
  - **Component integration**: The framework supports **standardized extension points** for plug-and-play services (e.g., analytics, control algorithms), with **runtime configuration management** enabling dynamic adjustments (e.g., adding/removing services) without full redeployment.
  - **Resource management**: Implicit **priority-based scheduling** for data flows (e.g., real-time control vs. batch analytics) and **scalability** from individual components to entire infrastructure systems, though no specific orchestration protocols (e.g., Kubernetes, Apache Kafka) are named.
  - **System-wide coordination**: The architecture bridges **static BIM representations** (via initial deployment) with **dynamic operational needs**, using the framework as a **unified hub** for data synchronization and service integration. No mention of distributed ledgers or blockchain for coordination."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the AECO (Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Operations) domain spans multiple phases, including **conceptualization, design, construction, operation, and decommissioning**, with a focus on **real-time synchronization** between physical assets and their digital counterparts. Representation types vary by phase: **BIM (Building Information Modeling)** is critical during design and construction for modular construction and infrastructure monitoring, while **sensor networks and IoT integrations** dominate operational phases for real-time data assimilation. Transition management between phases is hindered by **fragmented, case-specific deployments** lacking standardized data schemas, bidirectional communication protocols, and unified engineering workflows. Lifecycle governance approaches require **stakeholder alignment**, **cost modeling**, and **operational efficiency** optimization, but current implementations suffer from **high implementation costs** and **organizational barriers** such as siloed workflows and proprietary ecosystems (e.g., Bentley’s iTwin.js). Governance frameworks must address **interoperability**, **data consistency**, and **scalability** across the asset lifecycle, though no standardized governance model is explicitly detailed in the provided documents."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system addresses **fidelity and validity** by mitigating BIM’s static limitations through **real-time synchronization** between physical and virtual domains. Validation is implicitly supported by the framework’s **automated model transformations** and **runtime data exchange**, ensuring consistency between the twin and its physical counterpart. **Uncertainty sources** are not explicitly quantified, but the system’s reliance on **live operational data** (e.g., sensor inputs, control feedback) suggests dynamic error correction mechanisms. The **modular architecture** allows for **custom service integration**, enabling stakeholders to implement domain-specific validation logic (e.g., anomaly detection). While no explicit **error detection/correction mechanisms** or **quality assurance processes** (e.g., unit testing, recalibration) are detailed, the framework’s **life-cycle support** implies iterative refinement of models and data based on operational feedback. The **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric** is referenced as a conceptual validation approach, though specifics remain abstract."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT system employs a **modular architecture** with **standardized extension points**, enabling seamless integration of heterogeneous services and components. While specific **communication protocols** (e.g., MQTT, OPC UA) or **network topology** (e.g., star, mesh) are not detailed, the framework prioritizes **interoperability** across disparate systems, as outlined in the non-functional requirements (NF_X). **Scalability** is supported from individual components to entire infrastructure systems, with **low-overhead updates** ensuring maintainability. **Bandwidth and latency requirements** are not quantified, but the emphasis on real-time synchronization implies stringent performance constraints. **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are inferred from the system’s adaptability and bidirectional data exchange, though no explicit failover or backup protocols are described. **Security protocols** for network communication are not specified, though accessibility requirements suggest role-based access controls (RBAC) or similar mechanisms."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT system’s deployment infrastructure is centered around a **DT Framework** acting as a **central hub** for bidirectional data exchange and life cycle management. Hosting platforms (cloud/edge/on-premise) are not explicitly mentioned, but the framework supports **runtime configuration management**, allowing dynamic adjustments without full redeployment. Deployment models are tailored to the **AECO domain**, with roles including **Building Control Engineers** (responsible for device modeling and BIM tool integration), **Facility Managers** (interacting with deployed DTs for real-time monitoring), and **DT Engineers** (extending capabilities via service integration). Computational and storage requirements are implied by the system’s scalability needs, though specifics (e.g., resource allocation, storage tiers) are absent. **Availability and reliability** are addressed through the framework’s modular design and low-overhead update mechanisms, ensuring maintainability. Redundancy mechanisms or failover strategies are not detailed, but the emphasis on **interoperability** and **scalability** suggests resilience at the system level."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework prioritizes **interoperability across heterogeneous systems**, integrating with **BIM tools** (e.g., unspecified proprietary or open-source platforms) to enrich static building models with dynamic operational data. The system leverages **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** as part of its technical stack to facilitate **bidirectional data exchange** with external systems, though specific protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, REST APIs) are not detailed. Dependencies on external systems are managed through **standardized extension points**, ensuring compatibility with enterprise systems and other Digital Twins. The integration pattern is **requirements-driven**, addressing the AECO domain’s need to bridge static BIM representations with dynamic operational workflows, but lacks explicit mention of commercial BIM software (e.g., Revit, ArchiCAD) or granular protocol specifications."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "Standardization in AECO DTs is constrained by **lack of industry-specific standards compliance**, particularly for BIM integration. While frameworks like Eclipse Ditto and BaSyx adhere to **infrastructure-level standards** (e.g., IoT protocols), they are not designed for AECO use cases and require **manual adaptation**. Interoperability standards are not explicitly named, but the document highlights **fragmented deployments** and **proprietary ecosystems** (e.g., Bentley’s iTwin.js) as barriers. Certification requirements and compliance frameworks are not detailed, though **unified engineering workflows** and **data schemas** are identified as necessary for scalability. The absence of **open-source, AECO-optimized frameworks** exacerbates standardization challenges, as most existing solutions target industrial or IoT applications rather than construction-specific needs."
]
